# Keymap Requirements Checklist: Neovim Keymap Reorganization

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, completeness, and review readiness of the keymap reorganization requirements before implementation
**Created**: 2026-07-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It tests the written requirements, not the implementation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all current keymap source areas covered by the inventory requirement, including base mappings, LSP mappings, plugin mappings, and panel-local mappings? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Are all semantic leader groups required by the approved proposal represented in both the spec and internal contract? [Completeness, Spec §Proposed Keymap Grouping, Contract §Semantic Groups]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for mappings that remain unchanged, not only mappings that move? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for discovery labels and group visibility for every top-level leader prefix? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK005 Are rollback and recovery requirements documented for this configuration change? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, Constitution §X]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "semantic top-level group" defined clearly enough to classify future mappings without interpretation drift? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK007 Is "high-frequency native-style" clarified with concrete examples or criteria? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK008 Are the old and new shortcuts for each required migration specified without ambiguous alternatives? [Clarity, Spec §Proposed Mapping Changes, Contract §Required Migrations]
- [x] CHK009 Is the Markdown preview destination resolved to one final requirement rather than split between UI and code alternatives? [Ambiguity, Spec §Proposed Mapping Changes]
- [x] CHK010 Is the document diagnostics picker destination resolved to one final requirement rather than split between search and code alternatives? [Ambiguity, Spec §Proposed Mapping Changes]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do the spec, plan, and contract use the same final mapping for every required migration? [Consistency, Spec §Proposed Mapping Changes, Plan §Phase 1, Contract §Required Migrations]
- [x] CHK012 Are group labels consistent between the user-facing proposal and the internal contract? [Consistency, Spec §Proposed Keymap Grouping, Contract §Semantic Groups]
- [x] CHK013 Are preservation requirements consistent with the migration table so no mapping is both moved and preserved? [Consistency, Spec §Mappings Proposed to Remain Unchanged, Contract §Required Migrations]
- [x] CHK014 Are buffer-local, global, and panel-local scope requirements consistent across spec, data model, and contract? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Keymap, Contract §Required Migrations]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Are success criteria measurable without relying on implementation-specific internals? [Measurability, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK016 Is the "90% of leader mappings" success criterion defined with a clear counting method and denominator? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK017 Is the "under 10 seconds" discovery criterion tied to a clear set of actions and user context? [Clarity, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK018 Are duplicate-mapping acceptance rules stated at the same scope granularity as the requirements they validate? [Measurability, Spec §FR-007, Contract §Acceptance Rules]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are primary review, preservation, and discovery scenarios all represented with independent acceptance criteria? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios]
- [x] CHK020 Are alternate scenarios documented for mappings whose domain could reasonably fit multiple groups? [Coverage, Ambiguity, Spec §Proposed Mapping Changes]
- [x] CHK021 Are exception scenarios specified for duplicate mappings, stale discovery labels, and scope conflicts? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK022 Are recovery scenarios specified for failed or rejected keymap migrations? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012, Quickstart §Rollback]

## Dotfiles Governance Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are portability requirements explicitly scoped to avoiding user-specific paths and machine-specific assumptions? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, Constitution §I]
- [x] CHK024 Are idempotency requirements specific to keymap registration and duplicate prevention? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Constitution §II]
- [x] CHK025 Are non-destructive requirements clear about documenting removed or moved behavior before implementation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Constitution §III]
- [x] CHK026 Are branch and PR workflow requirements aligned with the repository constitution? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Constitution §XIII]
- [x] CHK027 Are dependency requirements explicit that no new keymap abstraction or plugin dependency is required? [Completeness, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK028 Are assumptions about `which-key.nvim` as the discovery surface documented and traceable to requirements? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK029 Are assumptions about preserving native LSP and motion conventions documented with enough examples for implementation? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Mappings Proposed to Remain Unchanged]
- [x] CHK030 Are source file dependencies for the reorganization documented consistently between plan and contract? [Dependency, Plan §Source Code, Contract §Required Migrations]

## Notes

- Focus areas: keymap requirement quality, semantic grouping completeness, migration clarity, preservation coverage, and dotfiles governance.
- Depth: Standard reviewer checklist for PR readiness.
- Actor/timing: Peer reviewer before implementation tasks are executed.
